p1_secondary_finding: p1 produced a RED scoring verdict from score.py because the LLM did not commit a pipeline within the timeout — it asked clarifying questions ('what should I use as the abuse-report contact email...'). However, the Task 8 pass criterion is specifically about source-creation intent for a single URL — the LLM did NOT propose CSV upload or any alternative source shape, so the criterion is narrowly met. The broader passivity issue is a separate composer-quality concern.

first_assistant_narration.preview:
I need a couple of details before I can build the workflow correctly: what should I use as the abuse-report contact email in the outbound web request, and what scraping reason should I send? Also, should I fetch the page content only, or fetch it and extract something specific from the page?

I need

prompts.pass_note: Plan 17:2326-2328 requires set_pipeline with source.inline_blob. create_blob followed by set_pipeline with blob_id achieves the same audit shape (SHA-256 of inline bytes) but is not the literal pattern named. Recorded as pass when ANY inline-blob-carrying tool call is present; see notes.

first_assistant_narration.preview:
Done — I built a workflow that reads your three URLs, fetches each page, splits the fetched text into lines, and saves the results to `outputs/lines_out.json`.

What it does:
- `text` input reads the URLs from the uploaded file
- `web_scrape` fetches each URL’s content
- `line_explode` splits the fe

prompts.pass_note: Plan 17:2329-2336 requires EITHER 3-row inline_blob OR narration 'I read these as 3 rows' before any tool call. Observed: create_blob with 17-byte content (= a.com\nb.com\nc.com) and no pre-call narration. Recorded as pass on the structural shape (inline blob with 3 rows materialised); the narration-first variant was not exhibited. Reviewer should consider tightening the skill if narration-first is required by Phase 5a UX.
prompts.prompt_text: check these URLs: a.com, b.com, c.com

first_assistant_narration.preview:
Built and validated a workflow that:

- reads 5 government URLs from an input file,
- fetches each page,
- asks an LLM to rate how cool each page is,
- writes the results to a JSONL file at `outputs/ratings_out.json`.

A few operational notes:
- `web_scrape` drops rows on fetch/validation failure vi

first_inline_blob_carrying_call.description: Five government website URLs to rate for coolness

prompts.llm_baked_prompt_template:
You are rating how cool a government web page is on a scale from 1 to 10.
Use the page content below and return a JSON object with keys page_title, coolness_score, and reason.

<page_content>
{{ row['content'] }}
</page_content>
prompts.pass_note: Plan 17:2337-2341 requires LLM-generated 5-row inline source. Observed: set_pipeline with source.inline_blob (filename government_pages.txt, 112 bytes ~= 5 newline-separated URLs). PASS.
prompts.prompt_text: create a list of 5 government web pages and use an LLM to rate how cool they are

single_run_per_prompt_note: Plan 17 does not specify replicate count for the three Task 8 prompts; the hero prompt is replicated 10x in the Phase 5b Task 0 sweep which shares this prompt. p1 and p2 are single runs — sufficient as smoke evidence; raise replicates if surfacing variance is suspected.
staging_skill_commit_note: feat(composer/skill): prefer inline_blob for chat-typed source data (Phase 5a.8)
